structure:
Line 1: Hook Context
Line 2: Bridge to Solution
Line 3: Proof/Benefit
Line 4: CTA
guide: Start with POV or That face when plus a relatable failure point. Keep it under 60 characters. Line 2 must name the app and state the core benefit in 3 to 5 words. Line 3 gives one concrete result with numbers if possible. Line 4 uses Comment KEYWORD or Save this. Total 125 to 220 characters.
example:
POV: You just missed your goal 30 days in a row 😱
So we built Smash to make showing up stupid simple.
Users hit 90 percent of goals in 21 days.
Comment GOALS for free link ↓

structure:
Line 1: Strong CTA
Line 2: Why they should act
Line 3: What they get
guide: Put the call to action in line 1. Use Comment, DM me, Save this. Line 2 explains the payoff in one sentence. Line 3 stacks the benefit. Use for warm audiences who already know the app. Keep under 200 characters.
example:
Comment GOALS for the link ↓
You asked for a goal app that stops you from quitting.
Smash tracks streaks and calls you out when you skip.

structure:
Line 1: Numbered hook
Body: 3 bullets or tips
Last line: Tie to app
guide: Start with a number like 3 reasons or 5 rules. Each bullet is one line with one insight about hitting goals. No fluff. Last line connects the value to your app. 200 to 500 characters total. Great for saves.
example:
3 reasons you keep failing your goals:
1. You track results not actions
2. You have zero accountability when motivation dips
3. You quit after missing 2 days
Smash fixes all 3 with daily check ins and streak repair.

structure:
Line 1: Open loop
Line 2: Hint not answer
Line 3: CTA to watch
guide: Create tension with a bold claim about goal achievement. Do not give the answer in text. Force them to watch the video. End with Watch till end or You need to see this. Keep under 150 characters.
example:
This one rule made me hit every goal in 2026 😱
See what happens at 0:06.
Comment GOALS if you want it.

structure:
Line 1: Relatable joke or POV
Optional Line 2: App punchline
guide: Use a common meme format or POV. Keep it under 50 characters if possible. Must be instantly relatable to people who set goals. Optional second line ties it to the app. Use when video carries 90 percent of the value.
example: Me vs the person I said I would be in January:

structure:
Line 1: Stat or quote
Line 2: Context
Line 3: CTA
guide: Start with a number like 18k users or a short testimonial quote. Line 2 explains what they achieved. Line 3 is Comment or Save. Builds trust for cold traffic.
example:
18,342 people hit their 30 day streak last month
They used Smash to stay consistent when motivation died.
Comment GOALS to join them ↓

structure:
Line 1: Yes, it's...
Line 2: Kill top objection
Line 3: Proof
Line 4: CTA
guide: Start with Yes, it's actually free or Yes, it takes 10 seconds. Line 2 addresses the main doubt people have about goal apps. Line 3 gives proof from the demo. Line 4 is CTA. Use when comments show skepticism.
example:
Yes, Smash is actually free 😅
No fluff. No 50 features. Just daily check ins.
Watch it take 5 seconds to log your day.
Comment GOALS to try ↓

structure:
Line 1: Before
Line 2: After
Line 3: How
Line 4: CTA
guide: Line 1 paints the painful before state. Line 2 shows the ideal after state. Line 3 says how the app bridges them. Line 4 is CTA. Keep each line short for readability.
example:
Before: Quit every goal by week 2
After: 100 day streaks on autopilot
How: Smash makes showing up non negotiable
Comment GOALS for free link ↓

structure:
Line 1: Time or scarcity hook
Line 2: What they lose
Line 3: CTA with deadline
guide: Start with Last day or Join 20k users before or While it's free. Line 2 states what happens if they miss out. Line 3 adds urgency to the CTA. Use sparingly or IG throttles it.
example:
Join 20k users before we add paywalls next month
Every day you wait is another goal you quit
Comment GOALS to lock in free access ↓

structure:
Line 1: Passage from book
Line 2: Book title plus author
Line 3: Concept breakdown
Line 4: Tie to app
Line 5: CTA
guide: Use a passage 50 to 100 words long from a productivity, habits, or goal book. It must tell a full story or explain a concept, not just a one line quote. Line 2 cites the book and author. Line 3 breaks down the idea in simple terms. Line 4 connects the concept to how Smash helps. Line 5 is Comment GOALS or Save this. 300 to 500 characters total. Use when video shows reading, planning, or deep work.
example:
When I wanted to change my life, I tried to change everything at once. I failed every time. Then I read that you do not need to be extreme, just consistent. Small actions done daily compound into massive results over time. The key is to never miss twice. One missed day is a mistake. Two missed days is the start of a new habit of quitting.
From Atomic Habits by James Clear
Consistency beats intensity. Your system matters more than motivation.
Smash is the system that protects your streak so you never miss twice.
Comment GOALS to start yours ↓
